body.u: I wanted to get an apprenticeship with a firm but I failed the interviews. I fortunately managed to get a job as a landscaper with a firm whose manager was the brother of a woman down the street I used to babbybaby sit for. It was quite skilled worked and I enjoyed it. It didn’t offer security but I was paid quite well. Landscaping was an expensive job of which more and more people done themselves. I got quite good at it and was promoted to a higher possisionposition. It was got fun working as a landscaper, you met people and you travelled. In 1981 I managed to by an old car and a house. My car brought me a lotslot of advantages. I could get about much easiliereasier which was a big help. I worked my way up to assitingassisting manager which was a bit of a break. This offered a bit more security and more money. I married in 1987 and settled down. It was a bit of a struggle but we didn’t live badly. I fathered my first child in 1989 and second in 1991. I did not have any more children than this because every family was only allowed two children because of the population problems. Because many people were getting paid more they wanted landscaped gardens, and so we were getting more business than ever which was very good.

My son and daughter grow up very quickly and my son the eldest managed to get an apprentishipapprenticeship with a firm. The school leaving age was now eighteen and the year was 2007. when he began his apprenticeship. I retired at 58 and lived on the savings and pension. I went fishing most days and they were the most enjoyable days of my life.
I am totooold now to go fishing and I just sit indoors waiting.
